meta.description: Compressed hunt cards for tokens — one-line detection rules for bulk scanning
meta.usage: Load cards into context. For each card, grep target code for card.grep. On hit: execute card.check steps directly, use validWhen/invalidWhen/impact for rapid triage, use optional preconditions/proofShape/falsePositiveSignals/reportEvidence when present, and only read full DB entry (card.ref + card.lines) for confirmed true/likely positives.

cards.title: 1. Transfer & TransferFrom Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: The ERC20 standard specifies that `transfer()` and `transferFrom()` should return a boolean indicating success.
check: VERIFY: Signal 1: External call (.call, .transfer, token transfer) occurs before state variable update; Signal 2: Token implements callback hooks (ERC-777,...
PROVE: Protocol calls token.transfer() or token.transferFrom(); Transfer fails silently (returns false or no return value)
FALSIFY: Not this bug when: Contract uses ReentrancyGuard (nonReentrant) on all entry points; Safe if: All state updates complete before any external call (strict CEI)
IMPACT: Silent transfer failures cause state/balance mismatches
Is SafeERC20 (OpenZeppelin) used for all token transfers?
If using Solmate, is contract existence checked for dynamic tokens?
cards.antipattern: USDT returns no value, this will revert or misbehave
cards.securePattern: Check contract exists before using Solmate
cards.validWhen: Signal 1: External call (.call, .transfer, token transfer) occurs before state variable update; Signal 2: Token implements callback hooks (ERC-777, ERC-721) and protocol...
cards.invalidWhen: Not this bug when: Contract uses ReentrancyGuard (nonReentrant) on all entry points; Safe if: All state updates complete before any external call (strict CEI)
cards.impact: Silent transfer failures cause state/balance mismatches
preconditions: attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern
Attacker exploits the state/balance mismatch to drain funds or cause DoS
cards.proofShape: Protocol calls token.transfer() or token.transferFrom() -> Transfer fails silently (returns false or no return value) -> Protocol assumes transfer succeeded and updates internal state
falsePositiveSignals: Not this bug when: Contract uses ReentrancyGuard (nonReentrant) on all entry points
Safe if: All state updates complete before any external call (strict CEI)
Requires attacker control of: specific conditions per pattern

cards.title: 2. Approval & Allowance Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Race Condition**: The `approve()` function doesn't protect against front-running when changing allowance from non-zero...
check: VERIFY: Race Condition: The approve() function doesn't protect against front-running when changing allowance from non-zero...
PROVE: Alice has approved Bob to spend 100 tokens; Alice sends TX to change approval to 50 tokens
FALSIFY: Prevents front-running by using relative changes
IMPACT: Double-spending via front-running
Does the protocol use increaseAllowance/decreaseAllowance?
Are USDT-like tokens (require approve 0 first) supported?
cards.antipattern: USDT requires approve(0) before setting new allowance
cards.securePattern: Prevents front-running by using relative changes
cards.validWhen: Race Condition: The approve() function doesn't protect against front-running when changing allowance from non-zero...
cards.invalidWhen: Prevents front-running by using relative changes
cards.proofShape: Alice has approved Bob to spend 100 tokens -> Alice sends TX to change approval to 50 tokens -> Bob sees pending TX and front-runs with transferFrom(alice, bob, 100)

cards.title: 3. Decimals & Precision Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Hardcoded 18 Decimals**: Using `1e18` or `10**18` as scaling factor regardless of actual token decimals
check: VERIFY: Hardcoded 18 Decimals: Using 1e18 or 1018 as scaling factor regardless of actual token decimals
PROVE: Precision loss due to division before multiplication
FALSIFY: Use battle-tested fixed-point library
IMPACT: Calculation results off by 10^12 or more
Does the protocol query token.decimals() for each token?
Are calculations normalized to a common precision?
cards.antipattern: Precision loss due to division before multiplication
cards.securePattern: Use battle-tested fixed-point library
cards.validWhen: Hardcoded 18 Decimals: Using 1e18 or 1018 as scaling factor regardless of actual token decimals
cards.invalidWhen: Use battle-tested fixed-point library
cards.impact: Calculation results off by 10^12 or more
cards.proofShape: Precision loss due to division before multiplication

cards.title: 4. Fee-on-Transfer Token Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Balance Tracking Mismatch**: Protocol tracks transferred amount instead of actual received amount
check: VERIFY: Balance Tracking Mismatch: Protocol tracks transferred amount instead of actual received amount
PROVE: Collateral amount recorded exceeds actual tokens held
FALSIFY: Full implementation with proper accounting
IMPACT: Contract balance becomes less than sum of all user balances
Does the protocol use balance-before/after pattern for deposits?
Are fee-on-transfer tokens explicitly supported or rejected?
cards.antipattern: Collateral amount recorded exceeds actual tokens held
cards.securePattern: Full implementation with proper accounting
cards.validWhen: Balance Tracking Mismatch: Protocol tracks transferred amount instead of actual received amount
cards.invalidWhen: Full implementation with proper accounting
cards.impact: Contract balance becomes less than sum of all user balances
cards.proofShape: Collateral amount recorded exceeds actual tokens held

cards.title: 5. Rebasing Token Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Cached Balance**: Storing balanceOf() result and using cached value later
check: VERIFY: Cached Balance: Storing balanceOf() result and using cached value later
PROVE: Using aToken amount directly without share conversion
FALSIFY: Track proportional shares, not absolute amounts
IMPACT: Cached balances become inaccurate after rebase
Does the protocol use share-based accounting?
Are rebasing tokens explicitly supported or rejected?
cards.antipattern: Using aToken amount directly without share conversion
cards.securePattern: Track proportional shares, not absolute amounts
cards.validWhen: Cached Balance: Storing balanceOf() result and using cached value later
cards.invalidWhen: Track proportional shares, not absolute amounts
cards.impact: Cached balances become inaccurate after rebase
cards.proofShape: Using aToken amount directly without share conversion

cards.title: 6. ERC777 Reentrancy Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Hidden Callbacks**: ERC777 transfers trigger hooks that execute arbitrary code
check: VERIFY: Hidden Callbacks: ERC777 transfers trigger hooks that execute arbitrary code
PROVE: Stake and immediately unstake via reentrancy
FALSIFY: Update state BEFORE external calls
IMPACT: Complete protocol drain possible
Do all functions follow Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern?
Is ReentrancyGuard applied to all state-changing functions?
cards.antipattern: Stake and immediately unstake via reentrancy
cards.securePattern: Update state BEFORE external calls
cards.validWhen: Hidden Callbacks: ERC777 transfers trigger hooks that execute arbitrary code
cards.invalidWhen: Update state BEFORE external calls
cards.proofShape: Stake and immediately unstake via reentrancy

cards.title: 7. Blacklist & Pausable Token Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Assumed Transferability**: Protocols assume transfers will always succeed
check: VERIFY: Assumed Transferability: Protocols assume transfers will always succeed
PROVE: Collateral permanently locked if user gets blacklisted
FALSIFY: Anyone can cancel orders for blacklisted makers
IMPACT: Transfer reverts block protocol functions
Does the protocol use pull-over-push pattern?
Can users specify alternative recipients for withdrawals?
cards.antipattern: Collateral permanently locked if user gets blacklisted
cards.securePattern: Anyone can cancel orders for blacklisted makers
cards.validWhen: Assumed Transferability: Protocols assume transfers will always succeed
cards.invalidWhen: Anyone can cancel orders for blacklisted makers
cards.proofShape: Collateral permanently locked if user gets blacklisted

cards.title: 8. Inflation & First Depositor Attack Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Share Price Manipulation**: First depositor can inflate share price by donating tokens
check: VERIFY: Share Price Manipulation: First depositor can inflate share price by donating tokens
PROVE: Standard share calculation without protection
FALSIFY: Track assets internally, ignore donations
IMPACT: First depositor can steal from all subsequent depositors
Does the vault use virtual shares/assets offset?
Is totalAssets() based on internal accounting (not balanceOf)?
cards.antipattern: Standard share calculation without protection
cards.securePattern: Track assets internally, ignore donations
cards.validWhen: Share Price Manipulation: First depositor can inflate share price by donating tokens
cards.invalidWhen: Track assets internally, ignore donations
cards.impact: First depositor can steal from all subsequent depositors
cards.proofShape: Standard share calculation without protection

cards.title: 9. Mint & Burn Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Missing Access Control**: Mint/burn functions without proper authorization
check: VERIFY: Missing Access Control: Mint/burn functions without proper authorization
PROVE: Burns shares but doesn't update internal accounting
FALSIFY: Prevent self-referential operations
IMPACT: Unlimited token supply creation
Are mint/burn functions properly access controlled?
Is there a maximum supply cap?
cards.antipattern: Burns shares but doesn't update internal accounting
cards.securePattern: Prevent self-referential operations
cards.validWhen: Missing Access Control: Mint/burn functions without proper authorization
cards.invalidWhen: Prevent self-referential operations
cards.proofShape: Burns shares but doesn't update internal accounting

cards.title: 10. Comprehensive Keyword Index
cards.detect: This section provides keywords optimized for vector search to help auditors find relevant vulnerability patterns.
check: VERIFY: This section provides keywords optimized for vector search to help auditors find relevant vulnerability patterns.
cards.validWhen: This section provides keywords optimized for vector search to help auditors find relevant vulnerability patterns.

cards.title: Summary & Quick Reference
cards.detect: Summary & Quick Reference
check: VERIFY: Summary & Quick Reference

cards.title: 1. First Depositor / Inflation Attack Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: The ERC4626 share calculation formula is:
check: VERIFY: ERC4626 share calculation formula is
PROVE: *Path A: [Classic First Depositor Front-Run]; Vault deployed with totalSupply = 0
FALSIFY: Use virtual shares to prevent inflation attack
IMPACT: Complete loss of depositor funds (0 shares minted)
Check if vault uses virtual shares (decimal offset)
Verify totalAssets() doesn't rely solely on balanceOf
cards.antipattern: totalAssets relies on balanceOf which can be manipulated
cards.securePattern: Use virtual shares to prevent inflation attack
cards.validWhen: The ERC4626 share calculation formula is
cards.invalidWhen: Use virtual shares to prevent inflation attack
cards.impact: Complete loss of depositor funds (0 shares minted)
preconditions: totalSupply is 0, shares are typically minted 1:1 with assets.
cards.proofShape: *Path A: [Classic First Depositor Front-Run] -> Vault deployed with totalSupply = 0 -> Attacker front-runs first legitimate depositor

cards.title: 2. ERC4626 Compliance Issues
cards.detect: ERC4626 specifies exact behavior for all functions including:
check: VERIFY: ERC4626 specifies exact behavior for all functions including
PROVE: Different logic for deposit vs mint on first deposit
FALSIFY: Same logic for first deposit in both functions
IMPACT: Integration failures with external protocols
Verify all 8 core functions are implemented (deposit, mint, withdraw, redeem + previews)
Check max* functions return 0 when operations disabled
cards.antipattern: Different logic for deposit vs mint on first deposit
cards.securePattern: Same logic for first deposit in both functions
cards.validWhen: ERC4626 specifies exact behavior for all functions including
cards.invalidWhen: Same logic for first deposit in both functions
cards.proofShape: Different logic for deposit vs mint on first deposit

cards.title: 3. Rounding Direction Vulnerabilities
cards.detect:
ERC4626 rounding rules:

- **Round DOWN** (favor vault): `convertToShares`, `previewDeposit`, `previewRedeem`

-...
check: VERIFY: ERC4626 rounding rules
PROVE: Mints 'amount' instead of 'shares'
FALSIFY: Mint the correct number of shares
IMPACT: Users can extract more assets than deposited
Verify convertToShares rounds DOWN
Verify previewDeposit rounds DOWN
cards.antipattern: Mints 'amount' instead of 'shares'
cards.securePattern: Mint the correct number of shares
cards.validWhen: ERC4626 rounding rules
cards.invalidWhen: Mint the correct number of shares
cards.impact: Users can extract more assets than deposited
preconditions: rounding favors the user instead of the vault, attackers can profit through rounding errors.
cards.proofShape: Mints 'amount' instead of 'shares'

cards.title: 4. Fee Handling Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Different fee calculations for deposit vs mint
check: VERIFY: Different fee calculations for deposit vs mint
PROVE: Doesn't account for underlying vault's deposit fees
FALSIFY: Check actual shares received and adjust accounting
cards.antipattern: Doesn't account for underlying vault's deposit fees
cards.securePattern: Check actual shares received and adjust accounting
cards.validWhen: Different fee calculations for deposit vs mint
cards.invalidWhen: Check actual shares received and adjust accounting
cards.proofShape: Doesn't account for underlying vault's deposit fees

cards.title: 5. Slippage Protection Issues
cards.detect: User has no control over minimum shares received
check: VERIFY: User has no control over minimum shares received
FALSIFY: Add deadline to prevent stale transactions
cards.antipattern: User has no control over minimum shares received
cards.securePattern: Add deadline to prevent stale transactions
cards.validWhen: User has no control over minimum shares received
cards.invalidWhen: Add deadline to prevent stale transactions

cards.title: 6. Exchange Rate Manipulation
cards.detect: Donation can inflate totalAssets
check: VERIFY: Donation can inflate totalAssets
PROVE: totalAssets() or _totalAssets() returns asset.balanceOf(address(this)) plus accounting variables.
FALSIFY: Track assets internally
LOOK FOR: `totalAssets()` or `_totalAssets()` returns `asset.balanceOf(address(this))` plus accounting variables.
cards.antipattern: Donation can inflate totalAssets
cards.securePattern: Track assets internally
cards.validWhen: Donation can inflate totalAssets
cards.invalidWhen: Track assets internally
cards.proofShape: totalAssets() or _totalAssets() returns asset.balanceOf(address(this)) plus accounting variables.

cards.title: 7. Decimal Handling Issues
cards.detect: Assumes all tokens have 18 decimals
check: VERIFY: Assumes all tokens have 18 decimals
PROVE: No scaling between different decimal tokens
FALSIFY: Handle decimals dynamically
cards.antipattern: No scaling between different decimal tokens
cards.securePattern: Handle decimals dynamically
cards.validWhen: Assumes all tokens have 18 decimals
cards.invalidWhen: Handle decimals dynamically
cards.proofShape: No scaling between different decimal tokens

cards.title: 8. Reentrancy Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Balance cached before transfer with ERC777 token
check: VERIFY: Balance cached before transfer with ERC777 token
FALSIFY: Global lock across related contracts
cards.antipattern: Balance cached before transfer with ERC777 token
cards.securePattern: Global lock across related contracts
cards.validWhen: Balance cached before transfer with ERC777 token
cards.invalidWhen: Global lock across related contracts

cards.title: 9. Token Compatibility Issues
cards.detect: Assumes transferred amount equals requested amount
check: VERIFY: Assumes transferred amount equals requested amount
FALSIFY: Revert if fee-on-transfer detected
cards.antipattern: Assumes transferred amount equals requested amount
cards.securePattern: Revert if fee-on-transfer detected
cards.validWhen: Assumes transferred amount equals requested amount
cards.invalidWhen: Revert if fee-on-transfer detected

cards.title: 10. Access Control & State Management
cards.detect: Returns max even when paused
check: VERIFY: Returns max even when paused
FALSIFY: max* reflects actual state
cards.antipattern: Returns max even when paused
cards.securePattern: max* reflects actual state
cards.validWhen: Returns max even when paused
cards.invalidWhen: max* reflects actual state

cards.title: 1.1 Using `transferFrom` Instead of `safeTransferFrom`
cards.detect: No check if receiver can handle ERC721
check: VERIFY: Using the basic transferFrom function instead of safeTransferFrom for ERC721 transfers does not verify whether the recipient can handle NFTs.
PROVE: No check if receiver can handle ERC721
FALSIFY: Validates receiver can handle ERC721
cards.antipattern: No check if receiver can handle ERC721
cards.securePattern: Validates receiver can handle ERC721
cards.validWhen: Using the basic transferFrom function instead of safeTransferFrom for ERC721 transfers does not verify whether the recipient can handle NFTs.
cards.invalidWhen: Validates receiver can handle ERC721
cards.proofShape: No check if receiver can handle ERC721

cards.title: 1.2 Using `_mint` Instead of `_safeMint`
cards.detect: No receiver validation
check: VERIFY: Minting NFTs with _mint() does not invoke the onERC721Received callback, potentially sending tokens to contracts that cannot handle them.
PROVE: Vault token minted without safety check
FALSIFY: Validates receiver before minting
cards.antipattern: Vault token minted without safety check
cards.securePattern: Validates receiver before minting
cards.validWhen: Minting NFTs with _mint() does not invoke the onERC721Received callback, potentially sending tokens to contracts that cannot handle them.
cards.invalidWhen: Validates receiver before minting
cards.proofShape: Vault token minted without safety check

cards.title: 2. Reentrancy via NFT Callbacks
cards.detect: State updated after callback
check: VERIFY: safeTransferFrom and _safeMint functions invoke onERC721Received on the recipient, creating a callback opportunity.
PROVE: Base token transfer before fractional balance update
FALSIFY: State updated before external call + reentrancy guard
cards.antipattern: Base token transfer before fractional balance update
cards.securePattern: State updated before external call + reentrancy guard
cards.validWhen: The safeTransferFrom and _safeMint functions invoke onERC721Received on the recipient, creating a callback opportunity.
cards.invalidWhen: State updated before external call + reentrancy guard
cards.proofShape: Base token transfer before fractional balance update

cards.title: 3. Approval and Access Control Issues
cards.detect: Approval persists across transfers
check: VERIFY: NFT approvals (single token or operator) are not revoked when the NFT is transferred, allowing previous approved addresses to reclaim the token.
PROVE: Approval persists across transfers
FALSIFY: Boolean parameter allows revocation
cards.antipattern: Approval persists across transfers
cards.securePattern: Boolean parameter allows revocation
cards.validWhen: NFT approvals (single token or operator) are not revoked when the NFT is transferred, allowing previous approved addresses to reclaim the token.
cards.invalidWhen: Boolean parameter allows revocation
cards.proofShape: Approval persists across transfers

cards.title: 4.1 Double Voting Through Self-Delegation
cards.detect: Self-delegation doubles voting power
check: VERIFY: When NFT holders first self-delegate, incorrect checkpoint logic allows them to double their voting power.
PROVE: Self-delegation doubles voting power
FALSIFY: Use delegates() function for actual delegate
cards.antipattern: Self-delegation doubles voting power
cards.securePattern: Use delegates() function for actual delegate
cards.validWhen: When NFT holders first self-delegate, incorrect checkpoint logic allows them to double their voting power.
cards.invalidWhen: Use delegates() function for actual delegate
cards.proofShape: Self-delegation doubles voting power

cards.title: 4.2 Delegation Disables NFT Transfers
cards.detect: Uses owner instead of delegate
check: VERIFY: After delegating votes away, the _afterTokenTransfer hook attempts to move votes from the token owner (who has 0 votes) instead of the delegate,...
PROVE: Uses owner instead of delegate
FALSIFY: Move votes between actual delegates
cards.antipattern: Uses owner instead of delegate
cards.securePattern: Move votes between actual delegates
cards.validWhen: After delegating votes away, the _afterTokenTransfer hook attempts to move votes from the token owner (who has 0 votes) instead of the delegate, causing underflow.
cards.invalidWhen: Move votes between actual delegates
cards.proofShape: Uses owner instead of delegate

cards.title: 4.3 Double Voting via Managed NFTs
cards.detect: Vote power transferable within epoch
check: VERIFY: Protocols with managed NFTs (holding multiple users' stakes) may allow voting power to be used twice when users withdraw from managed positions.
PROVE: Vote power transferable within epoch
FALSIFY: Update managed NFT vote state on withdrawal
cards.antipattern: Vote power transferable within epoch
cards.securePattern: Update managed NFT vote state on withdrawal
cards.validWhen: Protocols with managed NFTs (holding multiple users' stakes) may allow voting power to be used twice when users withdraw from managed positions.
cards.invalidWhen: Update managed NFT vote state on withdrawal
cards.proofShape: Vote power transferable within epoch

cards.title: 5. Royalty Manipulation
cards.detect: Royalty queried twice, can change between calls
check: VERIFY: When royalties are calculated twice (once for validation, once for payment), an attacker controlling the royalty recipient can change the fee between...
PROVE: Royalty queried twice, can change between calls
FALSIFY: Calculate and pay royalties in single pass
cards.antipattern: Royalty queried twice, can change between calls
cards.securePattern: Calculate and pay royalties in single pass
cards.validWhen: When royalties are calculated twice (once for validation, once for payment), an attacker controlling the royalty recipient can change the fee between calls.
cards.invalidWhen: Calculate and pay royalties in single pass
cards.proofShape: Royalty queried twice, can change between calls

cards.title: 6. ERC-721 Standard Compliance Issues
cards.detect: Returns value for non-existent tokens
check: VERIFY: ERC-721 standard requires tokenURI to revert for non-existent tokens.
PROVE: Overflow protection fails in edge case
FALSIFY: Reverts for non-existent tokens per EIP-721
IMPACT: Breaks composability with other protocols expecting standard behavior.
cards.antipattern: Overflow protection fails in edge case
cards.securePattern: Reverts for non-existent tokens per EIP-721
cards.validWhen: ERC-721 standard requires tokenURI to revert for non-existent tokens.
cards.invalidWhen: Reverts for non-existent tokens per EIP-721
cards.impact: Breaks composability with other protocols expecting standard behavior.
cards.proofShape: Overflow protection fails in edge case

cards.title: 7. NFT Liquidation and Collateral Issues
cards.detect: NFT stuck if owner blocks transfer
check: VERIFY: NFTs used as collateral may become permanently locked if liquidation or withdrawal mechanisms have bugs.
PROVE: NFT stuck if owner blocks transfer
FALSIFY: Users pull NFTs instead of automatic push
cards.antipattern: NFT stuck if owner blocks transfer
cards.securePattern: Users pull NFTs instead of automatic push
cards.validWhen: NFTs used as collateral may become permanently locked if liquidation or withdrawal mechanisms have bugs.
cards.invalidWhen: Users pull NFTs instead of automatic push
cards.proofShape: NFT stuck if owner blocks transfer

cards.title: 8. Self-Transfer Edge Cases
cards.detect: Self-transfer corrupts state
check: VERIFY: Self-transfers (from == to) may not be properly handled, causing state corruption in hooks or balance tracking.
PROVE: Self-transfer corrupts state
cards.antipattern: Self-transfer corrupts state
cards.validWhen: Self-transfers (from == to) may not be properly handled, causing state corruption in hooks or balance tracking.
cards.proofShape: Self-transfer corrupts state
